product: File arrival triggers with file events is now generally available
tagline: You can trigger job runs using file arrival triggers with file events on external locations, such as Amazon S3, Azure storage, or Google Cloud Storage.
what_it_does: You can trigger job runs using file arrival triggers with file events on external locations, such as Amazon S3, Azure storage, or Google Cloud Storage. To benefit from this feature, the external locations must have file events enabled. File arrival triggers with file events allow a higher trigger limit (1,000 per workspace) and removes the 10,000 files per trigger limit.
why_it_matters: How file arrival triggers work File arrival triggers make a best effort to check for new files every minute, although this can be affected by the performance of the underlying cloud storage.
plain_summary: You can trigger job runs using file arrival triggers with file events on external locations, such as Amazon S3, Azure storage, or Google Cloud Storage. To benefit from this feature, the external locations must have file events enabled.

capabilities.title: There no limits on the
capabilities.desc: There no limits on the number of files in the storage location.

capabilities.title: Triggers can error on time
capabilities.desc: Triggers can error on time out when there are too many extraneous file updates.

capabilities.title: A maximum of 50 jobs
capabilities.desc: A maximum of 50 jobs can be configured with a file arrival trigger on such locations in a Databricks workspace.

key_takeaway: You can trigger job runs using file arrival triggers with file events on external locations, such as Amazon S3, Azure storage, or Google Cloud Storage.
